content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). Its edges are numbered 111 through N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1. For each valid iii, there is an integer aia_iai‚Äã written on the iii-th vertex. Also, for each valid iii, there is an integer bib_ibi‚Äã written on the iii-th edge.

You want the following condition to be satisfied: for each vertex vvv and each edge eee adjacent to vvv, av‚â•bea_v \ge b_eav‚Äã‚â•be‚Äã. In order to achieve that, you may perform an arbitrary number of steps (including zero). In one step, you may perform one the following operations:


Select two different vertices uuu and vvv. Swap aua_uau‚Äã and ava_vav‚Äã.
Select two different edges eee and fff. Swap beb_ebe‚Äã and bfb_fbf‚Äã.
Select a vertex vvv and an integer xxx. Change ava_vav‚Äã to xxx and pay one coin.
Calculate the minimum number of coins you need in order to satisfy the required condition.
constraints: 1‚â§ui,vi‚â§N1 \le u_i, v_i \le N1‚â§ui‚Äã,vi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
the graph on the input is a tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àí11 \le i \le N-11‚â§i‚â§N‚àí1), the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äã, viv_ivi‚Äã and bib_ibi‚Äã denoting that the iii-th edge connects vertices uiu_iui‚Äã and viv_ivi‚Äã and the initial value written on it is bib_ibi‚Äã.
The last line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the minimum necessary number of coins.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There is no way to satisfy the required condition without paying any coins. When we have one coin, we can perform the following operations:

Swap the integers written on vertices 111 and 222.
Pay one coin and change the integer written on vertex 222 to 777.
